title: Networking and WISP Protocol
description: Establishing WISP tunnels, registering connections, writing/closing channels, managing workers and services

actors.name: Client Application
actors.description: JS code consuming PWispHandler or WorkersHandler APIs

actors.name: WISP Server
actors.description: Remote WebSocket server implementing the WISP protocol

actors.name: Puter Platform
actors.description: Auth, KV store, apps registry, and driver subsystem (puter.* globals)

use_cases.name: Establish WISP Tunnel
use_cases.description: Open WebSocket to wispURL; on CONTINUE(streamID=0) set _bufferMax and fire onReady; set up auto-reconnect on close

use_cases.name: Authenticate INFO Handshake
use_cases.description: On receiving INFO packet from server, send back INFO packet with puterAuth token if provided

use_cases.name: Register TCP Connection
use_cases.description: Allocate a new stream ID, store callbacks, send CONNECT packet to server
key_steps: ws.send(createWispPacket({ CONNECT, TCP, streamID, hostname, port }))

use_cases.name: Write Data to Stream
use_cases.description: Send DATA packet if buffer available; otherwise queue data for later flush

use_cases.name: Handle Buffer Flow Control
use_cases.description: On CONTINUE packet update stream buffer; flush queued data via _continue()
use_cases.trigger: Server sends CONTINUE packet for a stream
key_steps: _continue(streamID) → iterate queue, call write() for each item

use_cases.name: Receive Stream Data
use_cases.description: On DATA packet, deliver payload copy to registered dataCallBack

use_cases.name: Close Stream Channel
use_cases.description: Send CLOSE packet with reason 0x02 to terminate a stream

use_cases.name: Handle Remote Stream Close
use_cases.description: On server-initiated CLOSE, invoke registered closeCallBack
use_cases.trigger: Server sends CLOSE packet for a stream

use_cases.name: Create Worker
use_cases.description: Authenticate, resolve or create sandbox app, call driver 'create', store worker URL in KV

use_cases.name: Execute Worker
use_cases.description: Proxy a fetch Request to the worker, injecting puter-auth header

use_cases.name: List Workers
use_cases.description: Retrieve all worker file paths via driver call

use_cases.name: Get Worker
use_cases.description: Retrieve a single worker's info by name

use_cases.name: Delete Worker
use_cases.description: Destroy worker via driver, remove from KV store

use_cases.name: Stream Worker Logs
use_cases.description: Open WebSocket to logging endpoint, return EventTarget/ReadableStream-compatible log handle

use_cases.name: Register Service
use_cases.description: Instantiate service via factory, add to registry, trigger dependency-aware init

use_cases.name: Initialize Service with Dependencies
use_cases.description: Check if all dependencies are Running; if so init immediately, else queue as Pending until deps are ready
use_cases.trigger: maybe_init_(name) called after registration or dependency init
key_steps: Notify dependents via maybe_init_, fire init_listeners_

use_cases.name: Retrieve Running Service
use_cases.description: Synchronously get a Running service instance; returns undefined if not yet Running
key_steps: get(name): check status === Running, return instance

key_relationships: Client Application initiates all use cases in PWispHandler and WorkersHandler
WISP Server triggers UC02 (INFO handshake), UC05 (CONTINUE/flow control), UC06 (DATA receive), UC08 (remote close)
PWispHandler depends_on parsers.js: createWispPacket, parseIncomingPacket, packet type constants
WorkersHandler depends_on puter.* globals: authToken, ui, apps, kv, whoami
WorkersHandler depends_on utils.make_driver_method for all backend calls
WorkersHandler depends_on getAbsolutePathForApp for filePath resolution
WorkersHandler communicates_with Worker Service Backend via Puter driver framework
ServiceManager extends AdvancedBase, uses TService interface for get_depends/init
ServiceManager manages dependency graph: Pending → Initializing → Running lifecycle
ServiceManager notifies init_listeners_ on each service becoming Running
important_notes: PWispHandler uses WISP over WebSocket (binary arraybuffer). Stream IDs auto-increment from 1; streamID=0 is reserved for control (CONTINUE init, INFO auth). Buffer-based flow control: data queued when buffer=0, flushed on CONTINUE. WorkersHandler uses sandbox app pattern (sandbox-{workerName}) for user tokens. ServiceManager supports async dependency resolution with cascading init via waiting_ map.
